title: T-003 — skipOnboardingStep Server Action
prompt:
Implement the Server Action `skipOnboardingStep(orgId, step)` that pushes `step` onto `organizations.onboarding_state.skipped_steps[]` per PRD §14.3 (line 1532 — Skip button on optional steps).

Only steps 4 and 5 are optional per PRD §14.3 (First Product redirect, First Work Order redirect). The action MUST reject any other step number with typed error STEP_NOT_SKIPPABLE.

Also advances `current_step` to step+1 (or to 6 if step=5) and refreshes `last_activity_at`.

Do NOT bundle: state read/write actions (T-002), UI (T-008/T-009 use this action).

RED test command: `pnpm --filter web test -- skip-onboarding-step`.

pipeline_inputs.description: Skip handler for optional onboarding steps 4 and 5; advances current_step and records skip in skipped_steps[].
pipeline_inputs.details: Lives next to T-002 actions. Reuses same Zod schema. Validates step ∈ {4, 5} (only 'First Product' and 'First Work Order' are skippable per PRD). Idempotent: skipping the same step twice is a no-op (returns current state).
out_of_scope: UI calling this action (covered by T-008, T-009)
acceptance_criteria: Given current_step=4, when skipOnboardingStep(orgId, 4) runs, then skipped_steps=[4] AND current_step=5.
Given current_step=5, when skipOnboardingStep(orgId, 5) runs, then skipped_steps contains 5 AND current_step=6.
Given any current_step, when skipOnboardingStep(orgId, 1|2|3|6) runs, then result is typed error STEP_NOT_SKIPPABLE and DB unchanged.
Given skipOnboardingStep(orgId, 4) called twice, when second call returns, then skipped_steps still contains 4 exactly once and no error.
test_strategy: RED: vitest spec covering all 4 AC; expect failures pre-implementation.
risk_red_lines: Do not allow skipping non-optional steps (1, 2, 3, 6) — PRD §14.3 only steps 4 and 5 carry 'skippable'.
Do not push duplicates into skipped_steps[].
